overview.tagline: A diverse, family-friendly borough along the Hackensack River
overview.description: New Milford is a culturally diverse borough situated along the Hackensack River in central Bergen County. With a mix of residential neighborhoods, a growing business district, and easy access to major highways, New Milford offers an affordable alternative to neighboring communities while maintaining strong schools and active community programs. The borough is known for its diversity, with residents from a wide range of ethnic backgrounds contributing to a vibrant and welcoming atmosphere.
highlights: Culturally diverse community with residents from around the world
Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Hackensack River waterfront access and parks
Convenient access to Route 4, Route 17, and I-80

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Bergen County conducts periodic revaluations to ensure assessed values reflect market conditions.
realEstate.recentTrends: New Milford has seen steady price growth, driven by buyers seeking affordable Bergen County homes with good schools and highway access. The market has become more competitive in recent years with limited inventory. Multi-family properties and condos offer entry-level options.
neighborhoodNotes: Boulevard/River Road area — Close to the Hackensack River with river views
Main Street corridor — Walking distance to shops and restaurants
Henley Avenue area — Quiet residential streets with single-family homes
Columbia Road section — Established neighborhood near Bergenfield border
Madison Avenue area — Family-friendly streets near schools

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided by a contracted hauler for the Borough of New Milford.
details: Trash pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Containers must be at curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Maximum 50 lbs per container with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with DPW
Holiday collection schedule posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling collected on a regular schedule.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastics) collected biweekly
Paper and cardboard on alternate collection weeks
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through November
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Electronics recycling available at DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water (formerly United Water New Jersey). Sewer service is maintained through the Borough and the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Veolia Water provides water supply and billing
Sewer service through Bergen County Utilities Authority (BCUA)
Annual water quality reports available from Veolia
Emergency water issues handled 24/7 by Veolia
Sewer connection permits required for new construction

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department issues permits and oversees construction, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning Board reviews variance applications
Planning Board handles site plan and subdivision applications
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of occupancy required for new construction and major renovations

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages municipal parking lots and street parking regulations.
details: Municipal parking lots available in the downtown area
Commuter parking permits available for bus riders
Street parking subject to posted time limits
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking regulations enforced during storms

additionalServices.title: New Milford Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 200 Dahlia Avenue
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult events
ESL programs reflecting the borough's diverse population
Free Wi-Fi and computer access
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
details: Youth sports leagues, swimming, and summer camps
Adult fitness programs and senior activities
Community events and holiday celebrations
Online registration available

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: New Milford enforces noise regulations to protect the quality of life in residential areas.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Amplified music subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners are required to keep their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns and landscaping must be maintained
Properties must be free of debris and junk
Structures must be kept in good repair
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow within 24 hours
Vacant properties must be secured and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: All pets must be licensed and controlled per borough ordinances.
keyRules: Dogs must be licensed annually with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off owner's property
Pet waste must be cleaned up immediately
Excessive barking subject to complaints and fines
Cat licensing required

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking regulations are enforced throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking on streets prohibited from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM
Alternate-side parking enforced on certain streets for street sweeping
No parking within 25 feet of intersections or fire hydrants
Vehicles must be moved during declared snow emergencies
Parking violations subject to fines and towing

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental units must be registered and inspected per borough requirements.
keyRules: All rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of habitability required before occupancy
Annual registration renewal required
Landlords responsible for property maintenance to code standards
Multi-family dwellings subject to periodic fire safety inspections

other.title: Flood Zone Regulations
other.summary: Properties near the Hackensack River are subject to flood zone requirements.
keyRules: Construction in flood zones must meet FEMA elevation standards
Flood insurance required for properties in Special Flood Hazard Areas
Borough participates in the National Flood Insurance Program
Substantial improvements may trigger elevation requirements
other.source: Borough of New Milford Flood Prevention Ordinance

publicSchools.name: Berkley Street School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school for early elementary students

publicSchools.name: New Milford High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school with diverse student body and solid academic programs

privateSchools.name: Transfiguration Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving families in New Milford and surrounding communities

whyMoveHere: Affordable housing by Bergen County standards
Convenient access to Routes 4, 17, and major highways

commuteToNYC.notes: Several NJ Transit bus routes provide service to Port Authority Bus Terminal. The George Washington Bridge is accessible within 20 minutes by car. Some commuters use the nearby New Bridge Landing station area for alternate transit options.

costOfLiving.summary: New Milford offers relatively affordable living for Bergen County, with a range of housing options from condos to single-family homes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: At or near the New Jersey state average for overall cost of living
costOfLiving.vsNationalAverage: Above the national average, primarily due to property taxes and housing

pros: Affordable entry point into Bergen County
Good highway access for commuting in any direction
Solid public schools with diverse student populations
Active recreation programs and community events
cons: Some flood-prone areas near the Hackensack River
Downtown commercial area is modest compared to neighboring towns
Traffic congestion on River Road and Boulevard during rush hours
No direct train station — dependent on bus service
Property tax rates are above the county average

restaurants.name: Il Mulino
restaurants.description: Well-known Italian restaurant offering refined pasta dishes and seafood in an elegant setting.

restaurants.name: Sushi Palace
restaurants.description: Popular Japanese restaurant with an extensive sushi menu and hibachi options.

restaurants.name: El Unico Restaurant
restaurants.description: Authentic Latin American cuisine featuring Colombian and Peruvian dishes.

restaurants.name: New Milford Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving a full menu of American comfort food around the clock.

restaurants.name: Nori Sushi
restaurants.description: Modern Japanese restaurant with creative sushi rolls and Asian fusion dishes.

parks.name: Veteran's Memorial Park
parks.description: Central community park with sports fields, a playground, and memorial to borough veterans.

parks.name: Elmore Field
parks.description: Sports complex with ball fields and recreational facilities.

parks.name: Hackensack River Greenway
parks.description: Riverside walking path along the Hackensack River offering scenic views and nature observation.

parks.name: French Hill Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground equipment and open green space.

events.name: New Milford Community Day
events.description: Annual celebration featuring food, music, games, and family activities.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Festive ceremony with tree lighting, caroling, and holiday cheer.

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Community gathering honoring veterans at the Veterans Memorial Park.

attractions.name: New Bridge Landing Historic Park
attractions.description: Historic site at the nearby junction of the Hackensack River where George Washington's troops crossed during the Revolutionary War.

attractions.name: Hackensack River Waterfront
attractions.description: Scenic riverfront area offering walking paths, bird watching, and canoe/kayak access.

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the second and fourth Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: New Milford Borough Hall, 930 River Road
